title: Is Beauty Really in the Eye of the Beholder
subtitle: 美真的因人而异吗？科学说不一定

paragraphs.en: Statements like "beauty is in the eye of the beholder" are rarely questioned. They've become so embedded in our consciousness that people think it's absurd to think otherwise. It might be useful, however, to at least push back on this assumption because people evaluate environment, situations, and people aesthetically.
paragraphs.zh: 像“美在观看者眼中”这样的说法很少受到质疑。它们已深深植根于我们的意识之中，以至于人们觉得持不同想法简直荒谬。然而，对这一假设至少稍加反思或许不无裨益，因为人们确实会从审美角度评价环境、情境和他人。

paragraphs.en: We may find that we still believe it is correct, but we may also find that there is a lot more to the situation than you suppose. A recent study exploring aesthetic taste was published in Cognition. The results from this study show that people agree very much in their aesthetic evaluation of natural objects, but they disagree more about artifacts, or human-made objects.
paragraphs.zh: 我们或许会发现我们依然相信它是正确的，但也可能发现情况远比想象的要复杂。最近一项探讨审美品味的研究发表在《认知》期刊上。研究结果表明，人们对自然物象的审美评价高度一致，但对人工制品或人造物象的分歧则更大。

paragraphs.en: The study found that shared taste was most common for faces and natural landscapes, but least common among works of architecture and art. The hypothesis is the commonly pleasing features, like proportion or symmetry, are at work. Preferences for natural scenes might be learned through life experiences.
paragraphs.zh: 研究发现，共同的审美趣味在面孔和自然景观上最为普遍，而在建筑和艺术作品中最不常见。一种假设认为，诸如比例或对称等普遍令人愉悦的特征在起作用。对自然场景的偏好则可能是通过生活经历习得的。

paragraphs.en: Factors like habitability, safety, and openness might be preferred as people develop. Some of the details of landscapes change, but the basics are more common, e.g., water, open spaces, and signs of care. Art and architecture, unlike natural spaces, do not have the same level of exposure.
paragraphs.zh: 随着人的成长，宜居性、安全性和开阔感等因素可能会更受偏爱。景观的某些细节会发生变化，但基本要素更为共通，例如水、开阔空间和人工照料的痕迹。艺术与建筑则不同于自然空间，人们接触它们的程度并不相同。

paragraphs.en: So, people do not have the same level of shared taste. It's possible that the lower amount of agreement in the shared taste of artifacts has to do more with elements of style, rather than behavioral consequences. All of the consequences seem reasonable, but I think there might be an interesting philosophical conclusion that they did not draw.
paragraphs.zh: 因此，人们并没有同等程度的共同品味。人们对人工制品审美共识度较低，这可能更多与风格元素有关，而非“行为后果”所致。所有推论似乎都合乎情理，但我认为他们或许未能得出一个有趣的哲学结论。

paragraphs.en: It seems that there is an objective ground to our aesthetic preferences or evaluations. I am not saying this would mean beauty is completely objective, just that there could possibly be general objective principles at work, i.e., beauty is not simply in the eye of the beholder. Nature exhibits some of the universal aesthetic features, such as radiance, in a common way throughout the world.
paragraphs.zh: 看来，我们的审美偏好或评价是有客观依据的。我并非说这意味着美是完全客观的，只是说可能存在某种普遍的客观原则在起作用，也就是说，美并不仅仅存在于观看者的眼中。大自然以共同的方式在世界各地展现着某些普遍的美学特征，例如光辉灿烂。

paragraphs.en: For example, a sunset is similar enough in different places to warrant almost universal appeal. Possible universal principles of beauty — such as proportion, fittingness, radiance, and others — are general categories, which allow for a wide array of embodiments. When people get involved in making artifacts in architecture or art, they apply these very general concepts in unique ways.
paragraphs.zh: 例如，不同地方的日落都足够相似，因而几乎能获得普世的欣赏。比例、协调、光辉等可能的普世美学原则，属于一般性范畴，允许有各种各样的具体体现。当人们从事建筑或艺术创作时，他们以独特的方式运用这些极其普遍的概念。

paragraphs.en: But the way they applied the principle may not have universal appeal. This is why it's important to experience diverse cultures and their artifacts because it opens us up to different ways of approaching or constructing beauty. And it is always good to question our assumptions.
paragraphs.zh: 但他们运用这些原则的方式，却未必具有普世的吸引力。正因如此，体验多元文化及其产物才如此重要，因为它能让我们看到接近美或构建美的不同路径。而质疑我们自身的假设，也永远是一件好事。
